library_preparation_preflight.summary: Conscrypt OpenJDK uber has no Maven dependencies and bundles the native libraries, so no driver-applied dependency or Docker setup is needed. Meaningful coverage does require explicitly initializing/using the Conscrypt security provider; otherwise standard JCA/JSSE calls may exercise the JDK provider instead.
library_preparation_preflight.agent_guidance: In tests, explicitly create and use the Conscrypt provider, e.g. `Provider provider = Conscrypt.newProvider();` and pass it to JCA/JSSE factory methods such as `SSLContext.getInstance("TLS", provider)`, `Cipher.getInstance(..., provider)`, `Mac.getInstance(..., provider)`, or temporarily register it with `Security.insertProviderAt(provider, 1)` and remove it in cleanup. Include `Conscrypt.checkAvailability()` or provider creation early so native library loading is exercised.
risks: The artifact loads a bundled native library from `META-INF/native`; Native Image metadata must preserve the native resource/JNI access for runtime coverage.
If tests rely on unqualified `SSLContext.getInstance("TLS")` without provider registration, they may only cover the JDK default provider instead of Conscrypt.
Very new JDKs may warn or restrict native library loading, but JDK 21 execution should not require an extra system property.
